---
title: "For Parents"
---

Welcome! This space is dedicated to translating developmental science into practical insights for parents and creators. Here, we explore how stories and animation can help us navigate complex conversations with children.

::: {.panel-tabset}

## Translating Science
*Summaries of research written for families and media creators.*

### [Post 1] Why "Villains" Matter for Moral Growth
::: {layout="[30, 70]"}
![](images/villain_post_thumb.png)

Does watching "bad guys" help kids be good? We look at how morally ambiguous characters in animated films help children practice distinguishing between social rules and fundamental morals.
[Read the full post →](#)
:::

---

### [Post 2] Animation: A Tool for Tough Topics
::: {layout="[30, 70]"}
![](images/death_post_thumb.png)

Talking about death doesn't have to be scary. Learn how high-quality storytelling provides a "safe distance" for children to ask big questions about life and biology.
[Read the full post →](#)
:::

## External Resources
*A curated list of tools and guides from the broader developmental community.*

### For Conversations
* **Sesame Workshop**: Resources for talking to children about grief and difficult transitions.
* **Common Sense Media**: Age-based ratings and reviews focusing on the educational and moral value of films.

### For Media Creators
* **Center for Scholars & Storytellers**: Research-backed tip sheets for authentic representation of childhood in media.
* **The Fred Rogers Center**: Guidelines on using digital media in ways that support healthy relationships.

:::
---
